speaker: Adam
text: Good morning, Moiz! You won't believe this, but Israel just approved a law for public trials and the death penalty for detainees from October 7th.

speaker: Eve
text: Yeah, that’s pretty wild! Rights groups are already raising alarms about how this could strip away fair trial protections.

speaker: Adam
text: It’s such a heavy topic. Speaking of heavy situations, let's talk about Haiti. Gang violence in Port-au-Prince is forcing hundreds more people to flee their homes.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s heartbreaking. The clashes between rival gangs are escalating, creating a dire humanitarian situation. Residents are really facing tough choices right now.

speaker: Adam
text: Absolutely, and on a different note, Brazilian activist Thiago Ávila just returned to São Paulo after being detained in Israel.

speaker: Eve
text: Wow, that’s a significant story. Activism can really have some serious repercussions.

speaker: Adam
text: Moving on, the ex-mayor of a wealthy Los Angeles suburb admitted to acting as an agent for China. That’s a big deal, isn’t it?

speaker: Eve
text: It really is! He was allegedly promoting pro-China propaganda at the behest of Chinese officials.

speaker: Adam
text: And in Lebanon, residents in the south are refusing to flee again, even with escalating Israeli strikes.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s a testament to their resilience, but still, it must be terrifying for them.

speaker: Adam
text: For sure. Meanwhile, in Bolivia, a warrant has been issued for former President Evo Morales after he failed to show up for court.

speaker: Eve
text: Right? He’s facing some serious allegations, including fathering a child with a minor while in office.

speaker: Adam
text: Oh, and let's not forget about the hantavirus situation. The last passengers from a hantavirus-hit ship have been evacuated, but one American has tested positive.

speaker: Eve
text: That's alarming! Thankfully, the WHO is saying the risk to the public remains low, but still...

speaker: Adam
text: Definitely something to keep an eye on. Syria has made a move to restore credit card payments as part of efforts to rejoin the global economy.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s interesting! It seems like they’re trying to open up after years of isolation.

speaker: Adam
text: In Bolivia, protests are ramping up, with demonstrators blocking roads and demanding the president's resignation.

speaker: Eve
text: It’s a turbulent time for them, isn't it?

speaker: Adam
text: Switching gears, the Iran war situation continues to heat up. Trump has criticized an Iranian proposal as ceasefire talks hang by a thread.

speaker: Eve
text: It's a complex situation, with both sides playing a dangerous game.

speaker: Adam
text: And in Haiti, the Prime Minister has cast doubt on the possibility of a presidential vote happening by August due to the growing violence.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s concerning. Stability is crucial for any democratic process to take place.

speaker: Adam
text: Now, let's pivot to the markets. The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF, ticker VOO, closed up slightly by 0.22%.

speaker: Eve
text: There’s been some buzz around growth stocks lately, with many analysts suggesting a hold.

speaker: Adam
text: Right, and NVIDIA, ticker NVDA, saw a solid increase of nearly 2% and is rated a strong buy with a mean price target of about $269.

speaker: Eve
text: Their products continue to drive innovation in AI, so that’s no surprise.

speaker: Adam
text: Micron Technology, or MU, surged by 6.5%, also rated as a strong buy, with a mean price target around $573.

speaker: Eve
text: Chip stocks are really hot right now, aren’t they?

speaker: Adam
text: They are! Alphabet Inc., ticker GOOGL, closed down 3.03%, but it still carries a strong buy recommendation with a mean target of about $427.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s a bit of a dip, but it seems analysts still see a lot of potential there.

speaker: Adam
text: Meta Platforms, or META, also dropped a bit by 1.77%, but remains a strong buy with a target mean of $826.

speaker: Eve
text: There’s definitely a lot going on with their AI strategies that could affect their stock.

speaker: Adam
text: ASML Holding, ticker ASML, closed down 1.65%, still rated a strong buy, with a mean target of about $1,675.

speaker: Eve
text: Just shows how volatile these semiconductor stocks can be right now.

speaker: Adam
text: Taiwan Semiconductor, TSM, also dropped by 1.73%, with a mean target of about $463.

speaker: Eve
text: They have a lot of factors at play with supply chain issues and AI demand.

speaker: Adam
text: Cameco Corporation, ticker CCJ, closed up 2.9% with a buy rating and a mean target of around $138.

speaker: Eve
text: It seems like there’s a growing interest in nuclear energy again.

speaker: Adam
text: And lastly, Palantir Technologies, ticker PLTR, dipped slightly by 0.66%, still rated a buy, with a target mean of about $181.

speaker: Eve
text: They’ve been catching attention for their data analytics solutions.

speaker: Adam
text: Now, let’s move on to some new episodes in your podcasts. Twenty Thousand Hertz has a new episode titled "The Poop Song Conspiracy."

speaker: Eve
text: That one sounds quirky! It dives into a bizarre rabbit hole involving AI music and bathroom incidents. Definitely makes you curious!

speaker: Adam
text: Next up, 99% Invisible released "A History of the United States in 100 Objects." It’s about the stories behind everyday objects in American history.

speaker: Eve
text: Sounds fascinating! It’s always interesting to see history through a unique lens.

speaker: Adam
text: They also have another episode titled "Enshittification," discussing the challenges of fixing everyday items.

speaker: Eve
text: That title alone makes you want to know what it's all about!

speaker: Adam
text: People I (Mostly) Admire has an episode featuring Pete Docter, the chief creative officer of Pixar.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s a must-listen for animation fans! He discusses the creative process behind some of Pixar’s most beloved films.

speaker: Adam
text: And finally, Founders has an episode with Phil Knight, the founder of Nike, reflecting on his memoir "Shoe Dog."

speaker: Eve
text: Sounds like a great listen for entrepreneurs and sports fans alike!

speaker: Adam
text: That wraps up today’s news, Moiz.

speaker: Eve
text: Have a great day ahead, and drive safely!
